# @acepad/retry

A small utility to retry a function when it throws an error containing a `retryPromise`.

This package is designed to work with `@acepad/splashscreen`. If a thrown error has a `retryPromise` property, `retry` will wait for that promise (wrapped by `@acepad/splashscreen`) and then execute the function again.

## Installation

`bash
npm install @acepad/retry
`

## Usage

`js
import { retry } from "@acepad/retry";

async function loadData() {
  return retry(async () => {
    const res = await fetch("/api/data");
    if (!res.ok) {
      const err = new Error("Not ready");
      err.retryPromise = new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 1000));
      throw err;
    }
    return res.json();
  });
}
`

If the function throws an error without `retryPromise`, the error is rethrown.

## API

### `retry(p: () => T | Promise<T>): Promise<T>`

Executes `p`. If it throws an error with a `retryPromise` property, waits for that promise and retries once.
